@everyone English Version Notes：

Dear Keepers,

The global version of Morimens is approaching its 1.5-Year Anniversary! The B.I.A.V Team could not have made it this far without your great support. We are excited to introduce a series of optimizations in the upcoming 1.5 Anniversary Version Update. The preview will be divided into three parts: Combat Optimizations, System Optimizations, and Events & Rewards, with details coming in later announcements.

Please note: The following battle adjustments are still in the Final Beta phase. Further changes may be made based on feedback and testing. Final content will be confirmed in the official release.

[Awakener Soulforge Aptitude Enhancements]
Ramona, Lotan, Ogier, Doll, Aigis, and Erica will receive their own unique Soulforge Aptitude Enhancements, helping them play a stronger role in Astral Reign challenges.

[All Realms Posse Enhancement]
The Keyflare limit for all Realms will be increased to match Pure Chaos, allowing a second use of Special Posse each turn for more flexible planning. Pure Chaos' unique trait will be adjusted to: "The second use of Special Posse can be triggered twice," making Keyflare management even more crucial in this Realm.

[Aequor Realm Tentacle Enhancement]
A new effect is being added to Surging Tides Stance: If active at the end of a turn, a Tentacle will spawn at the start of the next turn. Additionally, once the Tentacle Limit is reached, further Tentacle generation will increase the limit, though the required number of generations will gradually rise. This change strengthens Tentacle-focused teams and further differentiates the three Aequor stances.

[Faded Legacy Difficulty Lowered]
The difficulty of Chapter 8 and the Final Chapter (Normal Mode) will be reduced, allowing players to clear them without relying heavily on meta teams. In the future, the most challenging combat content will be reserved for Hard Mode and the D-Effect Zone.

Gameplay is important, but it's not everything. For a game to be sustainable and provide ongoing experiences for players, it must choose the right approach to monetization. At first, My Turn adopted a business model similar to Clash Royale, but we failed. On the one hand, our team didn’t have the influence or the massive player pool that Supercell did, and on the other hand, My Turn’s gameplay was much more sensitive to numerical progression than Clash Royale. These two factors made that business model unsustainable. Although we shifted to a more balanced monetization strategy in China—adopting fair stats, replacing duplicate card draws with a talent system, and adding cosmetic skins, which gradually improved things—the funding eventually dried up, investors lost confidence, and we had no choice but to disband the team.

After taking over the development at Morimens, I paid special attention to this issue. Through the team’s collective effort, we were able to achieve so much as a small development group, bringing the game back from the brink of closing down. In hindsight, however, there are still many imperfections in monetization—otherwise, we might not have faced the “Resurrection competition” six months ago.

As for what we’ve learned in terms of gameplay, our current focus on PVE content in Traphase has been limited due to production constraints, but it’s precisely thanks to the development experience and foundation from My Turn that we could make rapid progress and avoid many detours. In a few months, we’ll be introducing entirely new character mechanics in Traphase that never existed in My Turn, and perhaps then we’ll truly learn the answer to this question.

In terms of gameplay design, The design principles of My Turn serve as the foundational basis for the PVE gameplay in Morimens. Of course, Slay the Spire has also been a huge inspiration—many of our team members greatly admire and respect it. The clearest lesson we took from there was how monster intent provides clear interaction and gives every turn a strong sense of purpose. But the concepts of team synergy, ultimate moves, and our underlying design model are rooted in the experience we gained from My Turn.

# `Realm Mastery`

```
For every 1 Realm Mastery...
Chaos: 
 - When activating a Posse, all Awakers gain an additional 0.05 Aliemus.

Aequor: 
 - When releasing an Exalt, there is an additional 0.25% chance to gain a stack of Tentacle Assembly. (If chance exceeds 100%, more stacks can be gained.)
 - The shield gained when switching to "Calm Sea" is increased by an additional 0.01% of Max HP.
 - The damage of the extra attacks during "Raging Waves" is increased by 0.02% of Tentacle Damage.
(Tentacle Assembly: Causes one Tentacle to attack at the end of turn.)

Caro:
 - The first Devour triggered each round grants an additional shield equal to 0.01% of Max HP, and temporary Power equal to 0.005% of Max HP.
 - This increases with amount of lost HP, up to a 100% increase.

Ultra:
 - At the start of turn, there is an additional 0.125% chance to add an [Inspiration] to your hand. (If chance exceeds 100%, more [Inspiration] cards can be gained.)```

-# Note: If your team is Pure Aequor, Pure Caro, or Pure Ultra - the Realm Mastery effects for those teams are doubled.
